document.title: Standard for Canned Crab Meat
document.label: CXS 90-1981 - Standard for Canned Crab Meat

sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This standard applies to canned crab meat. It does not apply to specialty products where crab meat constitutes less than 50% m/m of the contents.

sections.title: FOOD ADDITIVES
sections.text: Acidity regulators and sequestrants used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 09.4 (Fully preserved, including canned or fermented fish and fish products, including mollusks, crustaceans, and echinoderms) and only certain Table 3 acidity regulators and flavour enhancers as indicated in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.
sections.retrieval_text:
FOOD ADDITIVES
number=4
level=1
Acidity regulators and sequestrants used in accordance with Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in food category 09.4 (Fully preserved, including canned or fermented fish and fish products, including mollusks, crustaceans, and echinoderms) and only certain Table 3 acidity regulators and flavour enhancers as indicated in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in foods conforming to this Standard.

sections.title: HYGIENE
sections.text:
It is recommended that the products covered by the provisions of this Standard be prepared and handled in accordance with the appropriate sections of the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Practice for Fish and Fishery Products (CXC 52-2003), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Acid and Acidified Low-Acid Canned Foods (CXC 23-1979) and other relevant Codex Codes of Hygienic Practice and Codes of Practice.
The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CXG 21-1997).

The products should comply with any microbiological criteria established in accordance with the Principles and Guidelines for the Establishment and Application of Microbiological Criteria for Foods (CXG 21-1997).

sections.title: Product Definition
sections.text: Canned crab meat is prepared singly or in combination from the leg, claw, body and shoulder meat from which the shell has been removed, of any of the edible species of the sub -order Brachyura of the order Decapoda and all species of the family Lithodidae.

sections.title: Process Definition
sections.text: Canned crab meat is packed in hermetically sealed containers and shall have received a processing treatment sufficient to ensure commercial sterility.

sections.title: Presentation
sections.text:
Any presentation of the product shall be permitted provided that it:
(i) meets all requirements of this standard; and
(ii) is adequately described on the label to avoid confusing or misleading the consumer.

sections.title: Name of the Food
sections.text:
The name of the product shall be "crab" or "crabmeat".
In addition, the label shall include other descriptive terms that will avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.

sections.title: Sampling
sections.text:
(i) Sampling of lots for examination of the final product as prescribed in Section 3.3 shall be in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5. 
(ii) Sampling of lots for examination of net weight and drained weight where appropriate shall be carried out in accordance with an appropriate sampling plan established by the CAC.

sections.title: Sensory and Physical Examination
sections.text: Samples taken for sensory and physical examination shall be assessed by persons trained in such examination and in accordance with Annex A and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31 - 1999).

sections.title: Determination of Net Weight
sections.text:
Net weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedures: 
(i) Weigh the unopened container. 
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents. 
(iii) Weigh the empty container, including the end and any wrapping material, after removing excess liquid and adhering meat. 
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container and any  wrapping material from the weight of the  unopened container. The resultant figure is the net content.

page_spans.text:
Net weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedures: 
(i) Weigh the unopened container. 
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents. 
(iii) Weigh the empty container, including the end and any wrapping material, after removing excess liquid and adhering meat. 
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container and any  wrapping material from the weight of the  unopened container. The resultant figure is the net content.

source.document: CXS 90-1981 - Standard for Canned Crab Meat

reference.document: CXS 90-1981 - Standard for Canned Crab Meat

sections.title: Determination of Drained Weight
sections.text:
The drained weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedures: 
(i) Maintain the container at a temperature between 20C and 30C for a minimum of 12 hours prior to examination. 
(ii) Open the container and distribute the contents on a pre -weighed circular sieve having a wire mesh with square openings of 2.8 mm x 2.8 mm. 
(iii) Remove all wrapping material and incline the sieve at an angle of approximately 17-20 and allow the meat to drain two minutes, measured from the time the product is poured onto the sieve. 
(iv) Weigh the sieve containing the drained crab meat. 
(v) Determine the weight of drained crab meat by subtracting the mass of the sieve from the mass of the sieve with drained product.

page_spans.text:
The drained weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedures: 
(i) Maintain the container at a temperature between 20C and 30C for a minimum of 12 hours prior to examination. 
(ii) Open the container and distribute the contents on a pre -weighed circular sieve having a wire mesh with square openings of 2.8 mm x 2.8 mm. 
(iii) Remove all wrapping material and incline the sieve at an angle of approximately 17-20 and allow the meat to drain two minutes, measured from the time the product is poured onto the sieve. 
(iv) Weigh the sieve containing the drained crab meat. 
(v) Determine the weight of drained crab meat by subtracting the mass of the sieve from the mass of the sieve with drained product.

sections.title: Foreign Matter
sections.text: The presence in the sample unit of any matter, which has not been derived from crab meat, does not pose a threat to human health, and is readily recognized without magnification or is present at a level determined by any method including magnification that indicates non -compliance with good manufacturing practices and sanitation practices.

sections.title: Sensory and physical examination content
sections.citation: ab0a681e-59dc-4b73-8fce-833c9e7d7b66, Sensory and physical examination content, p. 5
sections.text:
1. Complete external can examination for the presence of container integrity defects or can ends which may be distorted outwards. 
2. Open can and complete weight determination according to defined procedures in Sections 7.3 and 7.4. 
3. Examine product for discolouration, foreign and objectionable matter.  
4. Assess odour, flavour and texture in accordance with the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CXG 31-1999).

sentences.original:
Net weight of all sample units shall be determined by the following procedures: 
(i) Weigh the unopened container. 
(ii) Open the container and remove the contents. 
(iii) Weigh the empty container, including the end and any wrapping material, after removing excess liquid and adhering meat. 
(iv) Subtract the weight of the empty container and any  wrapping material from the weight of the  unopened container.

prefilter.reason: Defines a measurement standard, typical for regulatory or quality control.

classification.sentence: The resultant figure is the net content.
classification.function: Definition of term resulting from procedure
classification.pattern: Simple declarative equating resultant figure with term
classification.justification: The sentence defines the meaning of a prior measurement result, lacking modality or communicative function typical of regulation commands; it's a simple definitional statement.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR CANNED CRAB MEAT
pages.summary: Página contém apenas o título principal do documento e cabeçalho, sem seções adicionais. Não há conteúdo interno ou subseções para mapear nesta página.

A página apresenta uma estrutura clara de seções numeradas e subseções detalhadas sobre o padrão para carne de caranguejo enlatada. Contém 6 seções principais numeradas (1 a 6) com várias subseções (como 2.1, 2.2, 3.1, 3.2, etc.). O texto associado a cada título foi devidamente distribuído respeitando a hierarquia e a continuidade da página.

A página apresenta duas seções principais numeradas 7 e 8 com subseções detalhadas (7.1 a 7.4 e 8.1 a 8.4). Cada subseção possui texto explicativo associado. O cabeçalho 'CXS 90-1981  3' foi identificado. A hierarquia está organizada em níveis 1 e 2, sem continuidade entre páginas marcada nesta página.

A página contém duas seções principais: 8.5 OBJECTIONABLE MATTER, continuando da página anterior, e 9 LOT ACCEPTANCE, ambas com seus textos explicativos. O cabeçalho da página foi identificado como 'CXS 90-1981  4'. Não há continuidade para a próxima página detectada.

A página possui um anexo intitulado 'ANNEX "A"' nível 1 e um subtítulo 'SENSORY AND PHYSICAL EXAMINATION' também nível 1 (por estar em caixa alta sem numeração). O conteúdo da lista numerada foi colocado em um nó textual separado para preservar a estrutura e assegurar clareza na associação do texto a seu respectivo título. Não há indicações de continuidade de outras páginas ou continuação para a próxima.
